summary: Renamed several HSD class-system functions and parameters from raw func_/arg names to descriptive class-library names, and propagated the newly identified symbols into assembly callsites and item code. The main symbol func_80382400 became hsdChangeClass via a small wrapper around hsdChangeClass_inline; func_80382854 became hsdDumpClassStat; KAR-derived placeholder helpers were replaced with names like class_set_flags, ForgetClassLibraryChild, and DumpClassStat. This was primarily a naming/readability PR for sysdolphin baselib class handling, with small asm and item callsite updates.
what_changed: Renamed func_80382400 to an inline implementation hsdChangeClass_inline plus public wrapper hsdChangeClass(void* object, void* class_info).
Updated assembly callsites in ftkirby.s, ft_unknown_006.s, ftparts.s, and grmaterial.s from bl func_80382400 to bl hsdChangeClass without changing instruction addresses or branch targets.
Updated src/melee/it/item.c to call hsdChangeClass(temp_r0, &lbl_803F1F90) and removed the old extern void func_80382400(HSD_MObj*, void*) declaration.
Renamed func_80420FBC to class_set_flags and renamed its parameters from arg1/arg2 to set/reset, clarifying that it clears reset bits and ORs set bits into class_info->head.flags.
Renamed func_804211B4 to ForgetClassLibraryChild and renamed name to library_name in class-library forgetting code.
Renamed func_804212F4 to DumpClassStat and renamed indent to level.
Renamed func_80382854 to hsdDumpClassStat and renamed parameters arg1/arg2 to recursive/level.
Updated asm/melee/text_2.s callsite from bl func_80382854 to bl hsdDumpClassStat.
smart_moves: Kept hsdChangeClass as a wrapper around hsdChangeClass_inline, preserving a concrete external symbol for existing assembly callsites while allowing the decompiled implementation to remain inline-shaped for matching.
Propagated renamed symbols into assembly immediately, improving global searchability and reducing future ambiguity around repeated func_80382400 callsites.
Renamed parameters based on behavior rather than guessing opaque names: set/reset for class_set_flags and recursive/level for hsdDumpClassStat directly describe the observed control flow.
Removed a stale local extern for func_80382400 from item.c once the clearer hsdChangeClass symbol was available.
decomp_lessons: For heavily shared baselib functions, rename the central implementation and then update asm callsites so future reverse-engineering sees semantic calls instead of isolated func_ labels.
When an address-derived or KAR-derived placeholder name is not useful, prefer a small behavior-based name such as class_set_flags or DumpClassStat if the function body is clear.
Wrapper-plus-inline can be useful when a function needs a stable external symbol for asm calls but the C body benefits from being inline for matching or structure.

Parameter names can carry important reverse-engineering knowledge even when types are still generic; library_name, recursive, level, set, and reset all document behavior without overclaiming unknown structures.
naming_conventions: HSD public/helper functions in this area use lower camel case with hsd prefix, e.g. hsdChangeClass, hsdIsDescendantOf, hsdObjIsDescendantOf, hsdForgetClassLibrary, hsdSearchClassInfo, hsdDumpClassStat.
Private/local helpers in class.c use descriptive PascalCase or snake_case-like mixed style present in the file, e.g. ForgetClassLibraryReal, ForgetClassLibraryChild, DumpClassStat, class_set_flags.
The public hsdChangeClass wrapper takes void* parameters while the inline implementation uses typed HSD_Obj* and HSD_ClassInfo*, reflecting looser external callsite typing.
Use library_name instead of generic name where the string is compared against head.library_name.
Use recursive and level for dump/tree traversal arguments instead of generic arg1/arg2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly-only files were updated only at symbol names on bl instructions; opcode bytes and addresses stayed the same, indicating a pure symbol-resolution rename.
The hsdChangeClass rename was applied to multiple callsites where r3 held an object/MObj-like pointer and r4 held class info, matching the new function semantics.
The hsdDumpClassStat rename was applied to a call with r3 = 0, r4 = 0, r5 = 1 in text_2.s, consistent with the C function's NULL-root/default-class behavior and level parameter.
The inline hsdChangeClass_inline preserves the original function body while hsdChangeClass provides the callable symbol needed by existing assembly references.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move hsdChangeClass or hsdDumpClassStat declarations into a header instead of using local prototypes.
Search for remaining func_80382400 or func_80382854 references after PR 495 to confirm the rename was fully propagated.
Investigate whether class_set_flags, ForgetClassLibraryChild, and DumpClassStat names were kept or later adjusted to match broader baselib naming conventions.
Look for additional HSD_ClassInfo flag helpers that should use set/reset parameter naming.
